faqs.question: How do I know if I have a drain fly infestation in my West Brighton home?
faqs.answer: Drain flies are small, moth-like insects often seen near sinks, showers, and floor drains. You may notice them resting on walls near bathrooms or kitchens, especially in the evening. They breed in the organic matter that accumulates in drains, so if you see multiple small flies hovering around your plumbing fixtures, you likely have an infestation that requires professional treatment.

faqs.question: What does the $350-$550 drain fly treatment include?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive drain fly treatment includes a thorough inspection of all drains and potential breeding sites, professional-grade bio-enzymatic treatments to eliminate organic buildup where flies breed, targeted application of safe insecticides when necessary, and detailed prevention recommendations. The price varies based on property size, severity of infestation, and number of drains requiring treatment.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take to work in West Brighton properties?
faqs.answer: You should see a significant reduction in drain fly activity within 2-3 days after treatment. Complete elimination typically takes 1-2 weeks as the treatment breaks down breeding material and the existing fly lifecycle completes. Our technicians will provide specific timelines based on your infestation severity and may schedule a follow-up visit if needed to ensure complete eradication.

faqs.question: Can drain flies return after treatment in West Brighton?
faqs.answer: While our treatment eliminates current infestations, drain flies can return if conditions allow new breeding. We provide detailed prevention guidance including regular drain cleaning, proper ventilation, and maintenance tips. Many West Brighton customers opt for our quarterly maintenance plans to prevent re-infestation, especially in older homes or properties with frequent plumbing use.
